projects.title: This Website
projects.summary: A responsive portfolio website showcasing my projects and experience.
projects.description: A portfolio website built to showcase my software development projects, technical skills and experience in a clean and accessible way. Rather than being a static page, the site is driven by a JSON project database, allowing new projects to be added without modifying the site's code. Each project page is generated dynamically and includes screenshots, descriptions, technologies used and links to source code or downloads where available. The website is fully responsive and designed to work well on both desktop and mobile devices. It also serves as a central hub for my programming portfolio.

projects.title: Film Tracker
projects.summary: Movie collection tracker powered by Google Sheets and TMDB.
projects.description: A website for tracking what films I have collected and what I have yet to collect. It pulls its data from a Google Sheet making use of the Sheets API. It also pulls information from The Movie Database (TMDB) in order to display a movie's poster, tags, runtime and release year. It also has searching functionality as well as options for changing the display order of the movies.

projects.title: All Alone
projects.summary: A Dialogue-Based Browser Game made in 3 Hours.
projects.description: All Alone is a dialogue-driven game made for the TriJam #317. The theme of the jam was "Missed Opportunity" and I only had 3 hours total to make it.

projects.title: Minecraft Modpack Maker
projects.summary: A Java Desktop Tool for making Minecraft Modpacks.
projects.description: Minecraft Modpack Maker is a Java desktop application designed to simplify the process of building complex Minecraft modpacks. The project was created to automate and/or simplify many of the repetitive tasks involved in assembling a modpack such as organising mods, generating the required folder structure, handling configs and preparing files for distribution. Although only a prototype, the project explored desktop application development, file management, user interface design and project organisation while also providing an intuitive interface for users who wanted to create modpacks without manually managing dozens of files.
